sn56.11:0.2: 2. The Chapter on Setting in Motion the Wheel of Dhamma
sn56.11:0.3: Setting in Motion the Wheel of Dhamma
sn56.11:1.1: At one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling near Bārāṇasī, at Isipatana in the Deer Park.
sn56.11:1.2: There the *Bhagavā* addressed the *bhikkhus* of the group of five:
sn56.11:10.1: But when, *bhikkhus*, my knowledge and vision of these four noble truths as they truly are, in this way with three turns and twelve aspects, was fully purified, then I claimed, *bhikkhus*, in the world with its *devas*, Māra, and Brahmā, in this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its *devas* and humans, to have awakened to the unsurpassed complete awakening.
sn56.11:10.2: And knowledge and vision arose in me:
sn56.11:10.3: ‘My liberation is unshakable; this is my final birth; there is now no renewed existence.’”
sn56.11:10.5: Delighted, the *bhikkhus* of the group of five rejoiced in the *Bhagavā*’s words.
sn56.11:11.1: And while this exposition was being spoken, the dust-free, stainless eye of *dhamma* arose in Venerable Koṇḍañña:
sn56.11:11.2: “Whatever is subject to arising, all that is subject to cessation.”
sn56.11:12.1: And when the wheel of *dhamma* [*dhammacakke*] had been set in motion by the *Bhagavā*, the earthbound *devas* made this proclamation:
sn56.11:12.10: the *devas* of Brahmā’s retinue made this proclamation:
sn56.11:12.11: “This unsurpassed wheel of *dhamma* [*dhammacakkaṁ*] has been set in motion by the *Bhagavā* near Bārāṇasī, at Isipatana in the Deer Park, and it cannot be turned back by any ascetic or brahmin or *deva* or Māra or Brahmā or anyone in the world.”
sn56.11:12.2: “This unsurpassed wheel of *dhamma* [*dhammacakkaṁ*] has been set in motion by the *Bhagavā* near Bārāṇasī, at Isipatana in the Deer Park, and it cannot be turned back by any ascetic or brahmin or *deva* or Māra or Brahmā or anyone in the world.”
sn56.11:12.3: Having heard the sound of the earthbound *devas*, the *devas* of the Four Great Kings made this proclamation:
sn56.11:12.4: “This unsurpassed wheel of *dhamma* [*dhammacakkaṁ*] has been set in motion by the *Bhagavā* near Bārāṇasī, at Isipatana in the Deer Park, and it cannot be turned back by any ascetic or brahmin or *deva* or Māra or Brahmā or anyone in the world.”
sn56.11:12.5: Having heard the sound of the *devas* of the Four Great Kings, the Tāvatiṁsa *devas* …
sn56.11:13.1: Thus, in that instant, at that moment, in that second, the sound rose up as far as the Brahmā world.
sn56.11:13.2: And this ten-thousandfold world-system shook, quaked, and trembled, and an immeasurable, magnificent radiance appeared in the world, surpassing the divine glory of the *devas*.
sn56.11:14.1: Then the *Bhagavā* uttered this inspired utterance:
sn56.11:14.2: “Koṇḍañña has indeed understood, sir; Koṇḍañña has indeed understood, sir.”
sn56.11:14.3: And so the name of Venerable Koṇḍañña came to be simply “Aññā Koṇḍañña.”
sn56.11:2.1: “These two extremes, *bhikkhus*, should not be followed by one gone forth.
sn56.11:2.3: This pursuit of sensual pleasure in sensual pleasures, which is low, crude, worldly, ignoble, and connected with harm; and this pursuit of self-torment, which is *dukkha*, ignoble, and connected with harm.
sn56.11:2.4: Without approaching both these extremes, *bhikkhus*, the middle way awakened to by the Tathāgata produces vision, produces knowledge, and leads to calming, direct knowledge, awakening, and *nibbāna*.
sn56.11:3.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is that middle way awakened to by the Tathāgata, which produces vision, produces knowledge, and leads to calming, direct knowledge, awakening, and *nibbāna*?
sn56.11:3.2: It is just this noble eightfold path, namely—
sn56.11:3.3: right view, right intention, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right *sati*, right *samādhi*.
sn56.11:3.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is that middle way awakened to by the Tathāgata, which produces vision, produces knowledge, and leads to calming, direct knowledge, awakening, and *nibbāna*.
sn56.11:4.1: Now this, *bhikkhus*, is the noble truth of *dukkha*—
sn56.11:4.2: birth is *dukkha*, aging is *dukkha*, illness is *dukkha*, death is *dukkha*, association with the unloved is *dukkha*, separation from the loved is *dukkha*, not getting what one wants is *dukkha*; in brief, the five aggregates subject to *upādāna* are *dukkha*.
sn56.11:4.3: Now this, *bhikkhus*, is the noble truth of the arising of *dukkha*—
sn56.11:4.4: it is this *taṇhā* that leads to renewed existence, accompanied by delight and passion, delighting here and there, namely—
sn56.11:4.5: *taṇhā* for sensual pleasures, *taṇhā* for existence, *taṇhā* for non-existence.
sn56.11:4.6: Now this, *bhikkhus*, is the noble truth of the cessation of *dukkha*—
sn56.11:4.7: the remainderless fading away and cessation of that very *taṇhā*, giving it up, relinquishing it, release from it, and non-reliance on it.
sn56.11:4.8: Now this, *bhikkhus*, is the noble truth of the way leading to the cessation of *dukkha*—
sn56.11:4.9: it is just this noble eightfold path, namely—
sn56.11:5.1: ‘This is the noble truth of *dukkha*,’ *bhikkhus*: in regard to *dhammas* unheard before, vision arose in me, knowledge arose, discernment [*paññā*] arose, true knowledge arose, light arose.
sn56.11:5.2: ‘Now this noble truth of *dukkha* is to be fully understood,’ *bhikkhus*: in regard to … arose.
sn56.11:5.3: ‘Now this noble truth of *dukkha* has been fully understood,’ *bhikkhus*: in regard to *dhammas* unheard before, vision arose in me, knowledge arose, discernment [*paññā*] arose, true knowledge arose, light arose.
sn56.11:6.1: ‘This is the noble truth of the arising of *dukkha*,’ *bhikkhus*: in regard to *dhammas* unheard before, vision arose in me, knowledge arose, discernment [*paññā*] arose, true knowledge arose, light arose.
sn56.11:6.2: ‘Now this noble truth of the arising of *dukkha* is to be abandoned,’ *bhikkhus*: in regard to … arose.
sn56.11:6.3: ‘Now this noble truth of the arising of *dukkha* has been abandoned,’ *bhikkhus*: in regard to *dhammas* unheard before, vision arose in me, knowledge arose, discernment [*paññā*] arose, true knowledge arose, light arose.
sn56.11:7.1: ‘This is the noble truth of the cessation of *dukkha*,’ *bhikkhus*: in regard to *dhammas* unheard before, vision arose in me, knowledge arose, discernment [*paññā*] arose, true knowledge arose, light arose.
sn56.11:7.2: ‘Now this noble truth of the cessation of *dukkha* is to be realized,’ *bhikkhus*: in regard to … arose.
sn56.11:7.3: ‘Now this noble truth of the cessation of *dukkha* has been realized,’ *bhikkhus*: in regard to *dhammas* unheard before, vision arose in me, knowledge arose, discernment [*paññā*] arose, true knowledge arose, light arose.
sn56.11:8.1: ‘This is the noble truth of the way leading to the cessation of *dukkha*,’ *bhikkhus*: in regard to *dhammas* unheard before, vision arose in me, knowledge arose, discernment [*paññā*] arose, true knowledge arose, light arose.
sn56.11:8.2: ‘Now this noble truth of the way leading to the cessation of *dukkha* is to be developed [*bhāvetabban*],’ *bhikkhus*: in regard to … arose.
sn56.11:8.3: ‘Now this noble truth of the way leading to the cessation of *dukkha* has been developed [*bhāvitan*],’ *bhikkhus*: in regard to *dhammas* unheard before, vision arose in me, knowledge arose, discernment [*paññā*] arose, true knowledge arose, light arose.
sn56.11:9.1: As long, *bhikkhus*, as my knowledge and vision of these four noble truths as they truly are, in this way with three turns and twelve aspects, was not fully purified, I did not claim, *bhikkhus*, in the world with its *devas*, Māra, and Brahmā, in this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its *devas* and humans, to have awakened to the unsurpassed complete awakening.
